findings.line: He looked as if he had not sat since yesterday.
findings.reason: `as if` weakens a direct exhaustion read; the sentence is padding an observation the scene already supports.
findings.suggestedFix: He looked worn to the limit.

findings.line: Uther's mouth moved as if he had nearly smiled.
findings.reason: `as if` turns a visible reaction into padded approximation instead of committing to the beat.

findings.line: Nobody spoke for a beat after that.
findings.reason: Temporal padding inserts dead air the silence does not need.

findings.line: He had probably heard men speak to the prince in many tones.
findings.reason: `probably` hedges an inference the prose is otherwise presenting as settled.
findings.suggestedFix: He had heard men speak to the prince in many tones.

findings.line: Only a harder line to the mouth, as if she had already put the answer to work.
findings.reason: `as if` softens an inference after the concrete facial detail has already landed.
findings.suggestedFix: Only a harder line to the mouth.

findings.line: Every sentence pointed toward one fact: the kingdom was feeding its own dead.
findings.reason: Abstract summary translates the concrete report the reader has just received.

findings.line: That earned the nearest thing to approval Sylvanas had yet seen in him.
findings.reason: Narration explains Arthas's reaction instead of showing a concrete approval beat.

findings.line: Every sentence pointed toward one fact: the kingdom was feeding its own dead.
findings.reason: The report paragraph closes with a thesis-restatement the preceding list already carried.
Paragraph-final caption closes the exchange by naming the tone instead of letting Uther's line carry it.

findings.line: He could have ordered her. He spent the choice instead.
findings.reason: The paragraph ends on interpretive closure instead of the offered choice itself.
findings.suggestedFix: He left the choice to her.

p5p6Sweep.notes: Dedicated second sweep checked inanimate and abstract subjects across the whole chapter. New kill remained line 215. Literal, collective, or subject-matter survivors included the town saving itself, the plague body mechanism, and the Light poised in Uther's hands.

findings.line: Arthas's eyes lifted from the map to her face for the first time.
findings.subject: eyes
findings.realAgent: Arthas looking up from the map
findings.reason: The body-part subject steals Arthas's volition; Arthas is the one acting.
findings.suggestedFix: Arthas looked up from the map to her face for the first time.

findings.line: how many bodies a city that size could hide before it stopped hiding anything at all.
findings.subject: city
findings.realAgent: the city's scale, density, and uncounted bodies inside it
findings.reason: The city is given concealment behavior that belongs to crowding, buildings, and people failing to keep count.
findings.suggestedFix: how many bodies could disappear into a city that size before anyone understood the count.

findings.line: Cyndia's eyes had gone to one of the quartermaster tables.
findings.subject: eyes
findings.realAgent: Cyndia looking toward the quartermaster table
findings.reason: The body-part subject launders the character's action into her eyes.
findings.suggestedFix: Cyndia was looking at one of the quartermaster tables.

audits.dominantPattern: B1 — Sentence too polished for the POV
audits.registerSummary: Sylvanas thinks in clipped tactical reads, rank judgments, and concrete operational pressure. She can be dry and sharp, but her interiority stays practical, not essayistic; she notices exits, speed, posture, and utility before thematic meaning.

findings.line: He led them between the tents fast enough to show what he had already chosen to leave undone for the next five minutes.
findings.reason: Sylvanas would register pace and triage pressure, but 'chosen to leave undone for the next five minutes' is polished conceptual paraphrase, not her in-scene shorthand.
findings.suggestedFix: He led them between the tents fast enough that the rest of the triage ground blurred past.

findings.reason: The fragment reads as a caption pasted over Uther's line, not an active tactical assessment Sylvanas is using.

findings.line: He could have ordered her. He spent the choice instead.
findings.reason: The line is thematically elegant and detached in a way that reads written-about rather than thought from inside Sylvanas's body.
findings.suggestedFix: He left the choice to her.

findings.line: An aide stood there helping sort route strips by district and gate mark, in plain campaign wool with dark hair damp at the temples and mud on the hem, built for camp anonymity.
findings.reason: The closing phrase sounds authored from outside the POV; Sylvanas would notice how easy he is to miss, not brand him with a thematic label.
findings.suggestedFix: An aide stood there helping sort route strips by district and gate mark, in plain campaign wool with dark hair damp at the temples and mud on the hem, easy to miss in the rush.

findings.line: The plate on his left shoulder had been cracked and buckled, not repaired, only strapped down to keep riding.
findings.reason: The negation bats away the repair state so the real image can land on the strapped-down armor.
findings.deletionTest: The plate on his left shoulder had been cracked and buckled, only strapped down to keep riding.
findings.suggestedFix: The plate on his left shoulder had been cracked and buckled, only strapped down to keep riding.

audits.cleanReason: Counted all seven tic families. marked=1 physical map use; tightened=1; eased=1 physical breath use; eye/gaze beats=4 total and at cap, not over it; corner-of-mouth, facial-shifted, weight/heaviness, and performance dialogue tags all stayed at 0. Nothing exceeded a chapter hard cap.

summary.verdictReason: 18 KILL verdicts across 4 guides (5 MODERATE+, 13 MILD). 18 have conservative suggested fixes cross-checked against all five guides.
